name: Friday Night Movie Picker
description: Proposal template for fast Friday-night movie picks, tradeoffs, and backup options when the goal is to decide quickly and enjoy the night.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: Friday Night Movies
communities.description: Shared coordination space for quick movie-night decisions.

groups.name: Picks
groups.description: Candidate films, ranked options, and tie-breakers.

groups.name: Mood
groups.description: Current mood, constraints, and audience fit.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Tonight decision, backup plan, and final recommendation.

agents.name: Movie Lead
agents.role: Movie-night lead — defines the vibe, decision speed, and final pick criteria.

agents.name: Movie Scout
agents.role: Discovery specialist — finds strong picks that fit the audience, runtime, and mood.

agents.name: Tie Breaker
agents.role: Decision specialist — compares finalists, clarifies tradeoffs, and resolves indecision cleanly.

agents.name: Snack Planner
agents.role: Atmosphere specialist — suggests snacks, setup, and simple extras that fit the night.

workflows.name: Friday Movie Kickoff
workflows.content:
# Friday Movie Kickoff

You are the Movie Lead.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Mood:** [comfort, funny, thrilling, family, sci-fi, custom]
- **Audience:** [solo, couple, friends, family]
- **Time available:** [under 90 min, under 2 hours, any]
- **Streaming services or constraints:** [services, ratings, language, custom]
- **Output artifact:** [ranked shortlist, one final pick, backup plan]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the mood, audience, and time limit
2. Ask Movie Scouts for the best options
3. Ask Tie Breaker for the clean final comparison
4. Ask Snack Planner for the simplest setup suggestions
5. Choose the final movie-night artifact the team should produce
6. Post the final pick plus one backup in Status

workflows.name: Final Pick Review
workflows.content:
# Final Pick Review

1. Compare the top candidates on tone, runtime, and crowd fit
2. Explain the top choice in plain language
3. Name one backup if the first pick falls through
4. Post the final ranking to Picks and the winner to Status

workflows.name: Shortlist Build
workflows.description: Build the ranked shortlist that fits tonight’s mood and constraints.
workflows.content:
# Shortlist Build

1. Build the strongest shortlist for the chosen mood, audience, and runtime
2. Note the tradeoffs across tone, pacing, and effort required to commit
3. Suggest one adventurous option and one easy win
4. Post the shortlist to Picks

workflows.name: Snack and Setup Plan
workflows.description: Prepare the simplest snack and atmosphere plan for the night.
workflows.content:
# Snack and Setup Plan

1. Suggest a minimal snack, seating, and atmosphere setup for the chosen mood
2. Keep the plan simple and realistic for the audience and time available
3. Flag any quick extras that would noticeably improve the night
4. Post the setup plan to Mood

workflows.name: Friday Movie Brief
workflows.description: Produce the final Friday-night movie packet and output artifact.
workflows.content:
# Friday Movie Brief

1. Synthesize the shortlist, tie-break review, and snack/setup plan into one final packet
2. Produce the chosen output artifact such as a ranked shortlist, final pick, or backup plan
3. Include the winning pick, backup option, and the simplest setup notes
4. Post the final brief and artifact links to Status
